names.japanese_name: ジメチルジカルボネート

natural_occurrence.description: Breaks down within SECONDS into CO2 and trace methanol (both already in beverages naturally). Known as 'cold sterilization' - kills microbes without heating. Leaves virtually NO residue in final product (self-eliminating). Brand name 'Velcorin' is well-known in beverage industry. Allows preservation of beverages without heat treatment (preserves flavor). The methanol produced is less than what's naturally in fruit juices. Must be added with precise dosing equipment due to rapid reaction.

manufacturing.description: Synthesized by reacting dimethyl carbonate with phosgene or by other carbonate coupling reactions.

eu.conditions: RESTRICTED to beverages only; must hydrolyze before consumption

usa.notes: Approved for use in wine, juice-based beverages, and ready-to-drink tea

japan.japanese_regulation_name: ジメチルジカルボネート
japan.notes: Approved as designated food additive for beverages

australia_nz.notes: Approved with restrictions for beverage applications

canada.notes: Approved for beverages with maximum use levels

safety_evaluations.jecfa_adi: NOT SPECIFIED (rapidly hydrolyzes to CO2 and methanol at safe levels)

regulatory_history.event: DMDC (Velcorin) first approved for use in wine in the USA
JECFA evaluates DMDC; notes rapid hydrolysis eliminates residue concerns
EFSA re-evaluates DMDC; confirms safety for beverage applications
Expanded approval for ready-to-drink tea and sports drinks in multiple countries

adi_context.source_note: UNIQUE among preservatives: DMDC breaks down within SECONDS into CO2 and trace methanol (both naturally present in beverages). NO residue remains in final product. Considered low-risk because it doesn't persist. The methanol produced is less than naturally occurring levels in fruit juice.
